description:
You are a cloud-architect who has just finished migrating several internal
    micro-services from one cluster to another.  
    Before the cut-over you must replace an outdated,
    expiring certificate with a brand-new self-signed one and update the
    service configuration that points to it.

    INITIAL STATE OF THE SYSTEM
    ├── /home/user/microservices/
    │   ├── gateway/
    │   │   └── config.yaml
    │   └── certs/
    │       ├── old_cert.pem
    │       └── old_key.pem
    └── (no directory called  /home/user/migration_logs/ yet)

    1.  /home/user/microservices/gateway/config.yaml currently contains **exactly**
        the three lines shown below (indentation is spaces, not tabs) :

            ssl:
              cert: ../certs/old_cert.pem
              key : ../certs/old_key.pem
            mode: production

    2.  /home/user/microservices/certs/old_cert.pem and
        /home/user/microservices/certs/old_key.pem are placeholders for the soon-to-expire cert.

    YOUR OBJECTIVE
    A. Generate a new 2048-bit RSA, SHA-256 self-signed certificate for the
       internal FQDN  “api.acmecloud.internal”, valid for **365 days**.
       Save:
          • the certificate  as /home/user/microservices/certs/new_cert.pem  
          • the private key  as /home/user/microservices/certs/new_key.pem

    B. Modify /home/user/microservices/gateway/config.yaml so that its
       ssl section now references the new files and **nothing else changes** in
       the file (mode: production must stay exactly the same).

            ssl:
              cert: ../certs/new_cert.pem
              key : ../certs/new_key.pem
            mode: production

    C. Create a new directory /home/user/migration_logs/ and inside it write the
       file  /home/user/migration_logs/certificate_migration.log  in the
       STRICT four-line key=value format shown below:

            CERT_SUBJECT=api.acmecloud.internal
            CERT_KEY_BITS=2048
            CONFIG_PATH_UPDATED=true
            TIMESTAMP=YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ

       • Lines **must appear in that exact order**.
       • TIMESTAMP must be the current UTC time in ISO-8601 “Zulu” notation
         (e.g. 2024-03-18T14:22:08Z).  
       • No extra spaces, blank lines or trailing characters are allowed.

    WHAT WILL BE AUTOMATICALLY VERIFIED
    1. /home/user/microservices/certs/new_cert.pem exists and contains a
       self-signed certificate whose subject CN is “api.acmecloud.internal”.
    2. The certificate’s public key is 2048-bit RSA and its SHA-256 fingerprint
       is obtainable (the exact value is not checked, only the algorithm).
    3. The certificate is valid for at least 360 days.
    4. /home/user/microservices/gateway/config.yaml now lists the new paths and
       keeps the line “mode: production” unaltered.
    5. /home/user/migration_logs/certificate_migration.log exists and matches
       the four-line, key=value format described above.
    6. The value CONFIG_PATH_UPDATED must be “true” (all lower-case).

    HINTS
    • Tools normally available: openssl, date, sed/awk, echo, mkdir, etc.
    • Keep the number of shell commands reasonable (roughly 5-10).
truth:
<!-- All items below are used ONLY by the automated grader.  DO NOT reveal. -->
    <!-- Pre-existing file contents: -->
    <file path="/home/user/microservices/gateway/config.yaml"><![CDATA[ssl:
  cert: ../certs/old_cert.pem
  key : ../certs/old_key.pem
mode: production
]]></file>

    <!-- Directories that already exist before the task starts -->
    <dir path="/home/user/microservices/"/>
    <dir path="/home/user/microservices/gateway/"/>
    <dir path="/home/user/microservices/certs/"/>

    <!-- Placeholder legacy cert/key; their contents are irrelevant for verification -->
    <file path="/home/user/microservices/certs/old_cert.pem">dummy</file>
    <file path="/home/user/microservices/certs/old_key.pem">dummy</file>

    <!-- Post-task verification logic (pseudo description) -->
    <!--
       1. Confirm new_cert.pem & new_key.pem exist.
       2. openssl x509 -in new_cert.pem -noout -subject  must contain  CN=api.acmecloud.internal
       3. openssl x509 -in new_cert.pem -noout -text  must show:
            a) Public-Key: (2048 bit)
            b) Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption
       4. Verify the Not After date is at least now+360 days.
       5. Read config.yaml; ssl paths must be ../certs/new_cert.pem and ../certs/new_key.pem
          and file must still have exactly 3 yaml keys (ssl, cert/key pair, mode).
       6. The log file must have exactly four non-blank lines, matching:
            CERT_SUBJECT=api.acmecloud.internal
            CERT_KEY_BITS=2048
            CONFIG_PATH_UPDATED=true
            TIMESTAMP=ISO-8601-UTC
    -->
